core_topic.definition: How professional photographers should optimize images on their websites for search engines — file formats, alt text, structured data, page speed, and discovery.
core_topic.audience: Working photographers running their own portfolio or business sites, semi-technical, want practical SEO advice without diving into developer-level detail.

pillars.title: Image SEO for Photographers: The Complete Guide
pillars.rationale: No existing pillar — GSC shows scattered impressions across 8 tangentially related posts but no comprehensive hub. DataForSEO shows 1,300 monthly volume on the head term with no dominant ranker; competitive opening.

pages.title: JPEG vs PNG vs WebP for Photography: Which Format to Use
pages.target_query: jpeg vs png vs webp photography
pages.notes: Comparison + decision-tree format = strong LLM citation potential. DataForSEO shows 890 monthly volume.

pages.title: How to Compress Images Without Losing Quality
pages.notes: GSC: 6,400 impressions / pos 11.2 — sitting just off page 1. Expand with more tools comparison, before/after examples, and side-by-side tests.

pages.title: WebP for Photographers: Should You Use It in 2026?
pages.notes: Format-specific guide. Browser support context, conversion tools, when not to use.

pages.title: How to Write Alt Text for Photography (with 20 examples)
pages.notes: Numbered-examples format is LLM-citation gold. Cover accessibility + SEO together.

pages.title: EXIF Data and SEO: Does It Matter?
pages.notes: Niche but genuinely useful. Answer the question definitively (mostly: no, but copyright EXIF matters).

pages.title: ImageObject Schema for Photographers: A Practical Guide
pages.notes: Show actual JSON-LD code. Schema markup pages tend to earn LLM citations because they're definitional.

pages.title: Image Sitemaps: Do Photographers Still Need Them?
pages.notes: Definitive answer page. Google's stance has evolved — current accurate guidance.

pages.title: How to Get Your Photos in Google Image Search
pages.notes: Strong head-term opportunity. DataForSEO shows 2,100 monthly volume. Step-by-step format.

pages.title: Google Lens for Photographers: What It Means for Your SEO
pages.notes: Emerging topic — fewer current authoritative pages, opportunity to be the definitive resource.

pages.title: Pinterest SEO for Photographers: Complete 2026 Guide
pages.notes: Pinterest is a major image-search destination for photography. Include account setup, board strategy, pin SEO.

rules: All cluster pages link back to the pillar within the first section, with anchor that names the pillar explicitly ('image SEO for photographers')
Cluster-1 (formats) pages cross-link liberally — readers comparing formats benefit from related comparisons
Tutorial pages link forward to the structured-data cluster as 'next step' content
The pillar links out to the strongest 2 pages per cluster — not every page

specific_recommendations.anchor_guidance: From the compression tutorial, link to the JPEG/PNG/WebP comparison as 'before compressing, choose the right format — here's how to decide'
From the Google Images guide, link to ImageObject schema as 'to give Google more context about your images, add structured data'
